newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Background flow predates the window: 2026 capex guided to upper end of $52-56B, >30% 2026 revenue growth guidance, CoWoS capacity modeled +89% YoY to 1.275M wafers (Goldman), and sell-side target raises (Bernstein, BofA $500, Aletheia $600). Today's -1.46% is mild drift, not fundamental; May monthly revenue report is due around June 10 and is the nearest catalyst.

sources.label: GuruFocus - TSM expects over 30% revenue growth in 2026
Investing.com - Goldman upgrades Taiwan packaging equipment stocks on TSMC CoWoS expansion
TSMC IR - 2026 monthly revenue calendar (May print due ~June 10)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new evidence since June 6 and yesterday's verdict was hold; on this crowding-capped lens TSM remains a fully discovered consensus AI winner, and a -1.46% drift day does not change the residual-alpha math at rank 36.
verdicts.bull: Structural monopoly on leading-edge AI logic with >30% 2026 revenue growth guided and capex at the top of the $52-56B range; even consensus names compound when execution is this clean.
verdicts.neutral: Everything bullish is already in the $2.2T market cap and 38-of-47 Buy ratings; alpha here is about positioning, not fundamentals, and positioning is maximal.
verdicts.bear: Crowded ownership plus +31.6% YTD means any AI capex digestion, Taiwan headline, or soft monthly revenue print de-rates the stock faster than fundamentals deteriorate.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: May 2026 monthly revenue report (~June 10) and any N2 ramp / CoWoS capacity updates
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding unwind on AI capex digestion or Taiwan geopolitical escalation

verdicts.rankRationale: This list explicitly penalizes global consensus holdings, and nothing since June 6 changes TSM's status as the most-owned name in the CPO/photonics supply chain; rank 78 already reflects that, so hold per yesterday's verdict.
verdicts.bull: TSMC's COUPE silicon photonics platform and CoWoS/CoPoS roadmap make it the manufacturing chokepoint for every CPO design, so it participates in the theme regardless of which optics vendor wins.
verdicts.neutral: Central but fully discovered: the CPO optionality is a rounding error inside a $2.2T market cap, so page-specific asymmetry is minimal either way.
verdicts.bear: As a consensus mega-cap it offers the least convexity on this list, and geopolitical/capex concentration risk is borne without the hidden-name upside the page is built to find.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Concrete COUPE/CPO production milestones or customer disclosures
verdicts.primaryRisk: Consensus positioning leaves no buyer of last resort in a derating; Taiwan concentration

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved this listing up on packaging scarcity, and no genuinely new CoWoS/packaging evidence has landed since June 6; the Goldman-modeled +89% 2026 CoWoS capacity path and advanced packaging passing 10% of revenue keep the thesis intact, so consolidate the move rather than chase it.
verdicts.bull: CoWoS capacity roughly doubling to 1.275M wafers in 2026 with demand still outstripping supply means TSMC controls the single tightest bottleneck in AI compute, and packaging revenue mix is rising.
verdicts.neutral: Packaging scarcity is real but already the consensus explanation for the YTD run; incremental score should wait for the May revenue print or a fresh capacity/pricing datapoint.
verdicts.bear: Aggressive CoWoS/CoPoS buildout plus panel-level packaging by rivals could turn today's scarcity into 2027 oversupply right as the stock's multiple peaks.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: May monthly revenue (~June 10) and CoPoS pilot-line completion targeted for June
verdicts.primaryRisk: CoWoS overbuild converting scarcity premium into excess capacity by 2027
